title: scanner: upsert_witness failure leaves note permanently unspendable
description:
scan_tx() calls insert_note() then upsert_witness() as separate non-atomic operations. If upsert_witness() fails after insert_note() succeeds, the note exists in DB but has no witness row. spendable_notes() uses INNER JOIN witnesses, so the note is permanently excluded from spendable balance. No recovery path.

## Algorithm
1. Open wallet/src/db.rs
2. Add new pub(crate) method insert_note_with_witness() after upsert_witness():
   Signature: pub(crate) async fn insert_note_with_witness(&self, note: &Note, block_height: u64, witness_data: &[u8]) -> Result<i64>
   - Begin a sqlx transaction: let mut txn = self.pool.begin().await?
   - Execute the same INSERT SQL as insert_note() but using &mut *txn as executor (copy the two-variant insert logic from insert_note())
   - Use sqlx::query!() against &mut *txn to get the note_id (LastInsertId)
   - Execute the same INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE as upsert_witness() using &mut *txn
   - txn.commit().await?
   - Return Ok(note_id)
3. Open wallet/src/scanner.rs, find scan_tx() at line ~438
4. In the match self.store.insert_note(&note).await branch:
   - Before the match, compute witness_bytes from the witness (same as current line ~508)
   - Replace the two-step call with: match self.store.insert_note_with_witness(&note, bh as u64, &witness_bytes).await
   - On Ok(note_id): insert into self.witnesses (same as current line ~515)
   - Err(e) if is_unique_constraint(&e): same debug log and continue
   - Err(e): return Err(e)
5. Remove the now-dead upsert_witness call and the standalone insert_note call in this arm

## Defensive programming rules
- The transaction must roll back automatically on any ? error before commit (sqlx semantics guarantee this)
- No unwrap() in the new method — all errors propagate via ?
- Do not log witness_data bytes (they are not key material but still opaque binary)
- insert_note() and upsert_witness() public signatures must not change (other call sites exist)
- The insert_note_with_witness method is pub(crate) only — not pub — no external visibility needed

## Acceptance
- cargo test -p nie-wallet passes including new test: insert_note_with_witness_is_atomic
  Test: call insert_note_with_witness(), verify BOTH note row and witness row exist in DB
  Oracle: direct DB queries after the call
- cargo clippy -p nie-wallet -- -D warnings passes
- No warn!(note_id, 'scanner: upsert_witness failed') path remains reachable in the Ok(note_id) arm of scan_tx()
